name: Modified Chlorophyll Absorption Ratio Index
formula: one of several CARI indices that indicates the relative abundance of chlorophyll
reference:
Daughtry, C., et al. "Estimating Corn Leaf Chlorophyll Concentration from Leaf and Canopy Reﬂectance." 
Remote Sensing Environment
 74 (2000): 229–239.
description:
Modified Chlorophyll Absorption Ratio Index (MCARI)


This index is one of several CARI indices that indicates the relative abundance of chlorophyll

name: Modified Red Edge Normalized Difference Vegetation Index
formula:
a modification of the 
Red Edge NDVI
  that corrects for leaf specular reflection
description:
Modified Red Edge Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (MRENDVI)


This index is a modification of the 
Red Edge NDVI
  that corrects for leaf specular reflection

name: Modified Triangular Vegetation Index
formula: similar to MTVI but is considered a better predictor of green LAI
reference:
Haboudane, D., et al. "Hyperspectral Vegetation Indices and Novel Algorithms for Predicting Green LAI of Crop Canopies: Modeling and Validation in the Context of Precision Agriculture." 
Remote Sensing of Environment
 90 (2004): 337-352.
description:
Modified Triangular Vegetation Index (MTVI)


This index makes TVI suitable for LAI estimations by replacing the 750 nm wavelength with 800 nm, whose reflectance is influenced by changes in leaf and canopy structures

name: Red Edge Normalized Difference Vegetation Index
formula:
a modification of the traditional broadband 
NDVI
description:
Red Edge Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (RENDVI)


This index is a modification of the traditional broadband 
NDVI

name: Transformed Chlorophyll Absorption Reflectance Index
formula: one of several CARI indices that indicates the relative abundance of chlorophyll
reference:
Haboudane, D., et al. "Hyperspectral Vegetation Indices and Novel Algorithms for Predicting Green LAI of Crop Canopies: Modeling and Validation in the Context of Precision Agriculture." 
Remote Sensing of Environment
 90 (2004): 337-352.
description:
Transformed Chlorophyll Absorption Reflectance Index (TCARI)


This index is one of several CARI indices that indicates the relative abundance of chlorophyll

name: Triangular Vegetation Index
formula: calculated as the area of a hypothetical triangle in spectral space that connects (1) green peak reflectance, (2) minimum chlorophyll absorption, and (3) the NIR shoulder
reference:
Broge, N., and E. Leblanc. "Comparing Prediction Power and Stability of Broadband and Hyperspectral Vegetation Indices for Estimation of Green Leaf Area and Canopy Chlorophyll Density." 
Remote Sensing of Environment
 76 (2000): 156-172.
description:
Triangular Vegetation Index (TVI)


This index is calculated as the area of a hypothetical triangle in spectral space that connects (1) green peak reflectance, (2) minimum chlorophyll absorption, and (3) the NIR shoulder

name: Atmospherically Resistant Vegetation Index
reference:
Kaufman, Y., and D. Tanre. "Atmospherically Resistant Vegetation Index (ARVI) for EOS-MODIS. 
IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
 30, no. 2 (1992): 261-270.
description:
Atmospherically Resistant Vegetation Index (ARVI)


This index was originally designed for use with MODIS

name: Modified Red Edge Simple Ratio
formula:
a modification of the broadband 
simple ratio (SR)
description:
Modified Red Edge Simple Ratio (MRESR)


This index is a modification of the broadband 
simple ratio (SR)

name: Red Edge Position Index
formula: a narrowband reflectance measurement that is sensitive to changes in chlorophyll concentration
reference:
Curran, P., W. Windham, and H. Gholz. "Exploring the Relationship Between Reflectance Red Edge and Chlorophyll Concentration in Slash Pine Leaves." 
Tree Physiology
 15 (1995): 203-206.
description:
Red Edge Position Index (REPI)


This index is a narrowband reflectance measurement that is sensitive to changes in chlorophyll concentration

name: Fast Line-of-sight Atmospheric Analysis of Hypercubes
description:
Fast Line-of-sight Atmospheric Analysis of Hypercubes (FLAASH)

reference: Penuelas, J., I. Filella, and J. Gamon. "Assessment of photosynthetic radiation-use efficiency with spectral reflectance."

name: Structure Insensitive Pigment Index
reference: Penuelas, J., F. Baret, and I. Filella. "Semi-Empirical Indices to Assess Carotenoids/Chlorophyll-a Ratio from Leaf Spectral Reflectance. "

name: Red Green Ratio Index
reference: Gamon, J., and J. Surfus. "Assessing Leaf Pigment Content and Activity With a Reflectometer."

name: Normalized Difference Nitrogen Index
value_range: from 0 to 1 will yield the most accurate result
reference: reflectance at 1680 nm, which should contain a similar signal due to foliar biomass, but without the influence of nitrogen absorption. The NDNI is experimental, but it does show strong sensitivity to changing nitrogen status when the canopy is green (not senescent) and closed in architecture. Applications include precision agriculture, ecosystem analysis, and forest management.
